item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Remember: The SCP Foundation is not a hotel.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: LOL, it has been long since last time I laughed so happily when reading an SCP. +1

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
This made me chuckle. Multiple times. Out loud. WHILE READING SOMETHING THATS NOT IN A MEME FORMAT!

Overall…1/10
The service on this article sucked I’ve been waiting for my food for an hour now.

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Oh hey, thanks a lot for your comment! Responding to the questions regarding the plot:

+ show block
– hide block

how the guy keeps escaping with such airtight conprocs

The current conprocs are very effective, so he doesn't escape anymore (even though he'll surely try, given the situation he's in), but at the beginning, the Foundation didn't know the whole specifics of his ability. In my mind, they set up this whole double containment chamber thing as soon as they realized that it'll be effective enough.

I know this is probably just a one-off, but I really hope we hear more from "the travel agency" in the future. An extradimensional GOI that sends people into odd and anomalous situations is a really fun idea.

At the moment, I don't have plans to write more about it, and/or make it a full-fledged GoI. Him mentioning this is kinda my attempt at world-building which would suggest that there are some scammers who tricked the poor guy, which led to this awkward situation. If someone else finds the idea of that agency interesting enough to write more of it, I certainly won't be against it.

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
I really like this.

In a sea of bulky 5kcon entries, this is a nice breather. I was thrown for a loop in the beginning, thinking it was going to be your standard humanoid anomaly sob story, but that lackluster first impression quickly devolved into intrigue, which in turn became amusement. The comedy of this article works really well. I especially liked how the incident logs reiterated on what is essentially the same joke while changing enough to keep it fresh each time.

Spoily Bits:

+ show block
– hide block

I know this is probably just a one-off, but I really hope we hear more from "the travel agency" in the future. An extradimensional GOI that sends people into odd and anomalous situations is a really fun idea.

My one question is how the guy keeps escaping with such airtight conprocs. If they were changed after the escape attempts then why weren't they changed as soon as they figured out that this guy could teleport?

All in all, it's a +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Just a thing : I'm surprised there isn't a note about how the Foundation searched for the website / travel agency after the interview.

I thought about it, but kinda decided that this detail would be pretty self-evident and not necessarily required within the interview log itself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The situation's kinda funny.

Just a thing : I'm surprised there isn't a note about how the Foundation searched for the website / travel agency after the interview.

+1 for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That's totally fair. I guess the reason for this is that the aim of some ideas here was more to imply the situation than to show it fully (like the last twist). Also, it was my concern to not let any of those elements to outstay their welcome.

Regarding the travel agency, I'm not sure whether it's worth expanding upon. But I guess if it is, it might as well be its own thing.

Thanks for the nitpicks too, off to fix those parts now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-4915 is a middle-aged male capable of short-range teleportation.

If the guy in the pic is middle-aged this makes me feel very old.

no visual or audible effects

I think this would read better as "visible or audible" (or "visual or auditory")

I feel like this doesn't go far enough in any particular direction to be as good as it could. There's a few things going on here, but none of them are as exciting as they could be - the absurd comedy of thinking the Foundation site is a hotel is cut short by the interview, and could be made a lot more bizarre - the same goes for this concept of the interdimensional travel agency. Alternatively, the dark humour/horror elements of this (the relative mundanity of booking a holiday and being locked up for it) are also not really run with very far.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think the ending wasn't as strong as it could've been. I dunno, "fucking hell!" just isn't a strong ending line for me. I am not exactly sure where else it could have left off. I did like the skip as a whole, though. And I liked him realizing he's not in a hotel. And it is, indeed, a bit comedic. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Actually, after a bit of consideration… I guess you're right. It's not necessary to explicitly state that detail. I guess it won't hurt the piece if I won't mention the nature of that agency.

So yeah, removed the "multiverse" part.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well…. yeah, I don't know about this one. I would've instantly upnoted, were it not for this phrase:

The multiverse travel agency promised me so much,

It really feels like forced exposition to me. I think that the existence of such an "agency" is implied in an excellent way throughout the article, and then you're just saying it out loud like that. A great deal of this article's fun aspect is coming exactly from 4915's insinuations, which leave the actual nature of the "agency" to each reader's imagination, so the ending just ruins the entire piece.

It's a novote, of course, but should you remove this phrase, I will change it to an upvote.

(Edit: Since the phrase in question has been removed, I've upvoted the piece)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks a lot!

wait a gosh darn minute

Yeah, they might've shown him something like 3408. I thought about this skip while writing it, but didn't crosslink because there's not much connection in terms of story.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is an extremely admirable exercise in character-building, especially given the rather silly conceit of the narrative. I empathize with this dude, like a lot. Not to mention the idea of accidental self-containment… damn.

But… But it was there, on the website. Five stars, all-inclusive, great stylized hotel themed after a research facility. And all those brochures, what they were all about then?

wait a gosh darn minute

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah. Imagine getting your money taken away for a trip that doesn't even exist, and ending up in containment because of it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh hey! I liked this.

And, as I said when we chatted about it earlier, fuckin' sucks to be that guy.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Too many walls, not enough entertainment. 1/10.

So, here's my first "comedic" SCP, which, ironically, is also based on human suffering just like most of my other SCPs. I thank the following cool people for feedback:

  • Cerastes
  • DianaBerry
  • cybersqyd
  • MalyceGraves
  • MaliceAforethought

Thanks a lot to you for reading as well!

Nearly forgot: Image Link
